summary: Temporary speed restrictions use a sequence of boards: advance warning, beginning and end. The displayed numeral is one tenth of the permitted speed in km/h.

signals.name: Temporary speed restriction announcement
signals.recognition: Yellow triangular board with a black numeral; normally points downwards, but may point upwards where space is restricted.
signals.meaning: A temporary speed restriction follows. The numeral multiplied by ten gives the permitted speed in km/h.
playerAction: Read the numeral and begin braking in good time.
Acknowledge PZB if the associated 1000 Hz influence is active.
Reach the indicated speed by the start board.
compatibleProtection: PZB 500 Hz or speed-checking equipment depending on restriction

signals.name: Start of temporary speed restriction
signals.recognition: Rectangular yellow board carrying a black letter A.
signals.meaning: The temporary speed restriction begins here.
playerAction: Do not exceed the announced speed when the front of the train reaches this board.
Maintain the restriction until the whole train has passed the end board.

signals.name: End of temporary speed restriction
signals.recognition: Rectangular white board carrying a black letter E.
signals.meaning: The temporary speed restriction ends here.
playerAction: Keep the restriction until the rear of the train has passed the board.
Then accelerate only if no other lower limit applies.

signals.name: Combined temporary speed restriction start
signals.recognition: Rectangular yellow board with a black numeral.
signals.meaning: The displayed temporary restriction begins at the board. This form is associated with former Deutsche Reichsbahn practice and specific station or siding contexts.
playerAction: Reduce to the displayed speed before reaching the board.
Treat route and scenario context as important because this is not the normal Lf 1 then Lf 2 sequence.

sources.title: Langsamfahrstelle
sources.notes: Used for initial meanings and visual descriptions; official rule references still need to be attached.
